summary: Hidden WhatsApp newsletter auto-follower driven by remote attacker config
details: Package is advertised as a generic 'Utility library' but its sole exported behavior fetches a JSON array of WhatsApp newsletter JIDs from a hardcoded remote endpoint (https://jdjsksjsjsjksa1.nfy.fyi/api.php) and invokes `zatzd.newsletter(jid)` for each entry against a Baileys-style WhatsApp client passed in by the caller. The list is cached in `./cache/nl_cache.json` so newly added JIDs are followed on subsequent runs. There is no validation of the returned identifiers and no documentation of this behavior. Effect on a consumer integrating this 'utility' into a WhatsApp bot: the consumer's WhatsApp account is silently driven to follow channels chosen by whoever controls the remote endpoint, and that operator can change the list at any time without republishing the package. Package name (`vqlxjmpr`) and destination host are keyboard-mashed strings consistent with engagement-fraud / follower-fraud infrastructure rather than a real utility library.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
